title: Strata #21 — Burgundy Silent Passage
summary: A vertical abstract painting from the Strata 2026 series, composed around a deep burgundy field crossed by pale incised fault lines. Small rose-toned monolithic forms create rhythm, silence and a sense of passage through a dense abstract territory.
concept: In Strata #21 — Burgundy Silent Passage, the surface unfolds as a quiet terrain shaped by subtle fractures and horizontal movement. The small geometric forms appear as signs of presence within the field, placed along lines that suggest routes, thresholds or geological memory. The painting evokes depth, stillness and the passage of time across the ground.
commercial_note: This work belongs to the Strata 2026 series, where abstract territory, fault lines and monolithic forms create a language of silence and structure. Its burgundy palette gives the piece a refined and contemplative presence, making it suitable for contemporary interiors, collectors and architectural spaces seeking depth and restraint.
